Hero.valueProposition: I translate technology into requirements — 10+ years in IT, now with an analytical focus.

Career.heading: My Path to Business Analyst
Career.intro: My professional path leads from technician to analyst — not a career change, but an arrival. In every role, I brought analytical thinking, coordination, and a big-picture perspective.

telekom.body: Fiber optic installation and network expansion — structured fault analysis and quality assurance as analytical foundation.
telekom.narrative: This is where I learned to systematically analyze complex technical systems — the basis for any requirements analysis.
telekom.technologies: FTTH, Fiber Optics, Splicing, Measurement Technology
details: Fiber optic installation and network expansion

mediacom.body: Coaxial network maintenance and signal analysis — data-driven fault diagnosis using MER and BER metrics.
mediacom.narrative: Signal analysis with concrete metrics was my first contact with data-driven decision making.
mediacom.technologies: Coaxial Networks, MER, BER, Signal Analysis

biermann.body: Planning and organizing civil engineering works, leading technician teams — project management and coordination in practice.
biermann.narrative: Team leadership, planning, and coordination of external partners — classic project management skills every business analyst needs.
biermann.technologies: Copper Network, Civil Engineering, Project Planning
details: Planning and organizing civil engineering works

bwi.body: IT infrastructure projects for the German Ministry of Defence — working with security-critical SINA systems and structured ticketing.
bwi.narrative: Security-critical systems, SINA experience, and structured processes — directly relevant to IAM at Bitmarck.
bwi.technologies: Cisco, LANCOM, VLAN, SINA, IBM Maximo
details: IT infrastructure projects for Ministry of Defence
System installation and configuration (Cisco, LANCOM, VLAN)

persona.body: IT project coordination and process optimization — leading small IT projects and coordinating external service providers.
persona.narrative: Short-term assignment focused on coordination and process optimization — analytical work under time pressure.

bhf.body: Developed CRM and training software — from concept through database design to productive operations.
bhf.narrative: First time owning the entire software development cycle — requirements analysis, architecture, implementation. The turning point toward analyst.
bhf.technologies: Node.js, PostgreSQL, REST APIs, UI/UX Design
details: Development of CRM and training software
Backend with Node.js and REST APIs

xecuro.role: IT System Administrator Secure Client Manager
xecuro.body: Administering and advancing SINA-based secure clients — contributing to security concepts.
xecuro.narrative: Deepening SINA expertise, actively shaping security concepts — the technical bridge to the IAM world.
xecuro.technologies: SINA, Windows, Linux, Hardening, Security Concepts
details: Administration and advancement of SINA-based secure clients
Hardening Windows and Linux systems under security policies
Collaboration with security and infrastructure teams

bitmarck.body: The logical consequence: Analytical thinking and technical understanding as an official role — shaping digital identities in healthcare.
bitmarck.narrative: All threads come together here — technology, analysis, security, coordination.

location.question: Location
location.answer: Flexible — remote and on-site both work. I'm mobile and currently based in the Cologne/Bonn area.

whyBitmarck.question: Why Bitmarck?
whyBitmarck.answer: Bitmarck digitizes healthcare — a domain with real impact. The Business Analyst IAM role connects my technical experience with analytical work on digital identities. Exactly what I'm looking for.

whyBA.question: Why Business Analyst?
whyBA.answer: Analytical thinking has been present in every one of my roles — from infrastructure planning to API architecture. Business Analyst makes this strength an official role.

Footer.builtWith: Built with Next.js, Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion, TypeScript, next-intl · Self-hosted with Docker

authApi.title: Auth API Service
authApi.description: Central authentication service with JWT, RBAC and Prisma — the core of my IAM experience.
authApi.problem: Multiple independent applications needed unified authentication and authorization. Each app had its own login logic — no central user management, no consistent permission model, no single sign-on.
authApi.approach: Self-designed RESTful auth service with Node.js and TypeScript. JWT token flow with access and refresh token rotation, role-based access control (RBAC) with fine-grained permissions, PostgreSQL with Prisma ORM. Clean API documentation and error handling.
authApi.result: Production-ready auth service centrally serving multiple applications. Use cases defined, data model designed, access concepts developed and implemented — completely independently from concept to deployment.
authApi.highlights:
Secure token rotation with access/refresh token pairs prevents session hijacking
Central service serving multiple applications with unified authentication
Fine-grained RBAC system with configurable roles and permissions
Clean API documentation with standardized error handling
authApi.learnings: This project fundamentally shaped my understanding of IAM architectures: token lifecycle management, access concepts, and the challenge of balancing security with usability — exactly the mindset a Business Analyst IAM needs every day.

nextCms.title: Next CMS
nextCms.description: Custom headless CMS with Next.js and Supabase — from client requirements to self-hosting.
nextCms.problem: Clients needed a flexible content management system without the complexity and cost of WordPress or Strapi. Requirements: simple operation, fast performance, self-hostable.
nextCms.approach: Headless architecture with Next.js frontend and Supabase backend (Auth, DB, Storage). Row-level security (RLS) for multi-tenant data separation. Auto-install script for one-click setup. Docker-based self-hosting on own server with automated backups.
nextCms.result: Fully functional CMS in production use by multiple clients. Requirements analysis conducted, architecture designed, frontend and backend independently implemented and documented.
nextCms.highlights:
Row-Level Security (RLS) for complete multi-tenant data separation at database level
Auto-install script for one-click setup without technical knowledge
Docker-based self-hosting with automated backups on own server
Production use by multiple clients with different requirements
nextCms.learnings: Requirements analysis for non-technical clients taught me to translate complex systems into simple usage concepts — a core competency for communication between business and technology.

myfitcoach.title: MyFitCoach PRO
myfitcoach.description: Fullstack coaching platform with Flutter, Next.js and Strapi — multi-platform with analytics.
myfitcoach.problem: Personal trainers and coaches needed a digital platform combining training planning, client management, progress tracking and communication in one app — mobile and desktop.
myfitcoach.approach: Multi-platform solution: Flutter mobile app for trainers and clients, Next.js web dashboard for administration and analytics, Strapi as headless backend. Realtime features for live updates, integrated analytics dashboard with progress charts and export functionality.
myfitcoach.result: Working coaching platform with iOS/Android app and web dashboard. Business requirements analyzed, multi-platform architecture designed and implemented across all layers.
myfitcoach.highlights:
Multi-platform architecture: Flutter mobile app, Next.js web dashboard, and Strapi backend
Integrated analytics dashboard with progress charts and export functionality
Realtime features for live updates between trainer and client
Role-based views for trainers, clients, and administration
myfitcoach.learnings: Stakeholder management across different user groups (trainers vs. clients) demonstrated how important it is to prioritize competing requirements and find sustainable compromises.

partnerApp.title: Partner App
partnerApp.description: Private social app with Flutter and Supabase — realtime chat, media sharing and end-to-end privacy.
partnerApp.problem: A private community needed a protected platform for communication and media exchange — focused on privacy, realtime interaction and seamless mobile experience. No third-party platform dependency.
partnerApp.approach: Native Flutter app (iOS/Android) with Supabase backend. Realtime chat via WebSockets, media upload with compressed previews, push notifications via FCM (Android) and APNs (iOS). Authentication with session management, content moderation and reporting system. GDPR-compliant data handling.
partnerApp.result: Production mobile app with active user base. Complete feature set independently designed: realtime messaging, media gallery, push notifications, user management and moderation tools.
partnerApp.highlights:
Realtime WebSocket architecture for low-latency messaging
Content moderation and reporting system for community safety
GDPR-compliant data handling with privacy-by-design approach
Production app with active user base and stable infrastructure
partnerApp.learnings: Privacy-by-design and moderation workflows sharpened my understanding of data protection requirements — from concept to technical implementation, directly transferable to GDPR compliance in healthcare.

logbuchApp.title: Logbuch App
logbuchApp.description: Mobile data capture with Flutter and Supabase — dynamic forms and structured export.
logbuchApp.problem: Field workers needed a mobile solution for structured data capture with flexible forms, offline capability and automated data export to existing systems.
logbuchApp.approach: Flutter app with Supabase backend, dynamic form engine (configurable fields and validations), offline sync mechanism and REST API integration for automatic data export. User-friendly UI with barcode scanner integration.
logbuchApp.result: Mobile data capture app in production use. Requirements gathered with end users, data model designed, form logic and export interfaces independently implemented.
logbuchApp.highlights:
Offline sync mechanism for reliable data capture without network connectivity
Barcode scanner integration for rapid product identification in the field
Dynamic form engine with configurable fields and validations
REST API integration for automated data export to existing systems
logbuchApp.learnings: Requirements gathering with field users showed how important it is to understand real working conditions — requirements emerge in the field, not at the desk.

mpaNutrition.title: MPA Nutrition Shop
mpaNutrition.description: Custom e-commerce system — payment processing, shipping logic and marketing in one package.
mpaNutrition.problem: A nutrition company needed their own online shop without dependency on Shopify or WooCommerce. Requirements: full control over design, payment flow and customer data.
mpaNutrition.approach: Custom shop system from scratch: product catalog with variants, cart with session persistence, payment integration (Stripe), shipping logic with tracking, customer accounts with order history. Marketing tools (discount codes, newsletter) and integrated analytics dashboard.
mpaNutrition.result: Functional online shop with complete order flow in production. Business requirements analyzed, UX designed and full-stack independently implemented.
mpaNutrition.highlights:
Stripe payment integration with complete checkout flow and error handling
Shipping logic with tracking and automatic status updates
Marketing tools: discount codes, newsletter system, and analytics dashboard
Customer accounts with order history and session-based cart
mpaNutrition.learnings: Analyzing e-commerce business processes — from cart through payment to shipping — sharpened my understanding of end-to-end process modeling and business requirements.
